sn.s: Roberto organiza una juntada en su casa.
sn.e: Roberto organizes a get-together at his place.

sn.s: "Vení, maje, va a estar tuani," me dice.
sn.e: "Come, dude, it's gonna be awesome," he tells me.

sn.s: Llego y el ambiente está deacachimba.
sn.e: I arrive and the vibe is awesome.

sn.s: Hay birras, música, y mucho relajo.
sn.e: There are beers, music, and lots of fun.

sn.s: "¡Ideay, chele! Pensé que no venías," grita alguien.
sn.e: "Hey, foreigner! I thought you weren't coming," someone shouts.

sn.s: "¡Aquí ando, pues! No me lo iba a perder."
sn.e: "Here I am! I wasn't gonna miss it."

sn.s: Bailamos, contamos chistes, la pasamos bien.
sn.e: We dance, tell jokes, have a good time.

sn.s: "Este chele ya chivea como nica," se ríen.
sn.e: "This foreigner already parties like a Nica," they laugh.

sn.s: "¿Nos desvelamos hasta el amanecer?" propone Roberto.
sn.e: "Shall we stay up till dawn?" Roberto proposes.

sn.s: Entre amigos, el tiempo no importa.
Ya no soy invitado: soy parte de la juntada.
sn.e: I'm not a guest anymore: I'm part of the crew.

sn.s: Pertenecer se siente así, en confianza y relajo.
sn.e: Belonging feels like this — in trust and good fun.
